name: Denmark
contextBlurb: Denmark applies the EU’s MiCA regime to stablecoins. Tokens fall under EMTs (fiat-pegged) and ARTs (basket-referenced), with Finanstilsynet as the national competent authority for all MiCA titles. MiCA requirements (already in force EU-wide) cover authorisation, governance, 1:1 liquid reserves and par-value redemption for EMTs, plus robust disclosures and supervision for ARTs. A Danish tax reform on crypto—including possible mark-to-market taxation from 2026—has been proposed by the Tax Law Council but not enacted; the government postponed the bill for further consultation. Separately, Danske Bank joined a nine-bank EU consortium planning a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin for H2-2026; this is a pan-EU initiative and not an authorisation of a Danish EMT issuer.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
End of Denmark’s MiCA CASP transitional period (18-month grandfathering). After this date, only authorized CASPs may operate.
DAC8 crypto-asset reporting begins in Denmark; providers must report 2026 transactions to SKAT (first exchange in 2027).
Finanstilsynet authorizes Eurodollar ApS as an e-money token (EMT) issuer under MiCA (USD€).
Remaining MiCA provisions (incl. CASPs) apply across the EU, completing the core regime beyond ARTs/EMTs.
MiCA Titles III–IV for ARTs/EMTs apply EU-wide, bringing reserve, redemption and disclosure rules for stablecoins into force.

keyFigures.name: Danish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finanstilsynet)
keyFigures.role: National competent authority under MiCA. Licenses and supervises CASPs and relevant token issuers, and oversees AML/CFT

keyFigures.name: Danish Tax Agency (Skattestyrelsen)
keyFigures.role: Administers tax rules for crypto-assets (including gains/losses, reporting). Not a prudential regulator.

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Denmark applies the EU’s MiCA regime to stablecoins. Tokens fall under EMTs (fiat-pegged) and ARTs (basket-referenced), with Finanstilsynet as the national competent authority for all MiCA titles. MiCA requirements (already in force EU-wide) cover authorisation, governance, 1:1 liquid reserves and par-value redemption for EMTs, plus robust disclosures and supervision for ARTs. A Danish tax reform on crypto—including possible mark-to-market taxation from 2026—has been proposed by the Tax Law Council but not enacted; the government postponed the bill for further consultation. Separately, Danske Bank joined a nine-bank EU consortium planning a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin for H2-2026; this is a pan-EU initiative and not an authorisation of a Danish EMT issuer.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Under MiCA, Denmark classifies stablecoins as Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) (pegged 1:1 to a single official currency) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs) (referencing a basket of assets/values). EMTs must be issued by a credit or e-money institution authorised by Finanstilsynet, with e-money-style p

regulators.name: Danish Financial Supervisory Authority
regulators.role: National competent authority under MiCA. Licenses and supervises CASPs and relevant token issuers, and oversees AML/CFT for financial sector entities.

regulators.name: Danish Tax Agency
regulators.role: Administers tax rules for crypto-assets (including gains/losses, reporting). Not a prudential regulator.
